summary: The implementation fails to resolve a persistent bandit:B603 finding across three attempts. The core issue is that subprocess.run receives untrusted user input (job_name, params values, env values) without adequate validation: job_name is not checked for path traversal, params values are not sanitized, and env values are not validated against the required alphanumeric/underscore/hyphen pattern. The security fixer attempts did not address these validation gaps, resulting in a fixer loop without resolution.
suggested_actions: Validate job_name to prevent path traversal by ensuring it contains no path separators or parent directory references
Sanitize params values by restricting to allowed characters (alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen) or using a whitelist approach
Validate env values against the required pattern (alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen) as specified in the constraints
Consider using a list-based subprocess call with explicit argument separation to avoid shell injection
Add input validation for all user-supplied fields before constructing the subprocess command

evidence.description: subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input.
